# Work Packages: Semantic Resources Phase 1

**Inputs**: Design documents from `/kitty-specs/001-semantic-resources-phase1/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, contracts/, quickstart.md

**Tests**: Included as integral part of implementation — Expecto + YoloDev.Expecto.TestSdk.

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package must be independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `/tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`.

---

## Work Package WP01: Project Scaffolding (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Create all new .fsproj files, configure NuGet dependencies, wire project references, and update the solution file.
**Independent Test**: All new projects build successfully with `dotnet build`.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP01-project-scaffolding.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~350 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-001

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T001 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Frank.Cli.Core.fsproj` with dependencies (dotNetRdf.Core, dotNetRdf.Ontology, dotNetRdf.Shacl, FSharp.Compiler.Service, Ionide.ProjInfo, Ionide.ProjInfo.FCS)
- [x] T002 [P] Create `src/Frank.Cli/Frank.Cli.fsproj` with PackAsTool=true and System.CommandLine dependency
- [x] T003 [P] Create `src/Frank.Cli.MSBuild/Frank.Cli.MSBuild.csproj` as pack-only targets project
- [x] T004 [P] Create `src/Frank.LinkedData/Frank.LinkedData.fsproj` with dotNetRdf.Core dependency and Frank project reference
- [x] T005 Create `test/Frank.Cli.Core.Tests/Frank.Cli.Core.Tests.fsproj` and `test/Frank.LinkedData.Tests/Frank.LinkedData.Tests.fsproj` with Expecto + TestSdk
- [x] T006 [P] Create `sample/Frank.LinkedData.Sample/Frank.LinkedData.Sample.fsproj`
- [ ] T007 Update `Frank.sln` to include all new projects

### Implementation Notes
- Follow existing Frank conventions: multi-target net8.0;net9.0;net10.0 for libraries, net10.0 for test/sample projects.
- Frank.Cli.Core and Frank.Cli may need single-target net10.0 due to FCS/Ionide.ProjInfo compatibility.
- Reference `src/Directory.Build.props` for shared packaging properties.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T002, T003, T004, T006 can proceed in parallel once T001 establishes the pattern.

### Dependencies
- None (starting package).

### Risks & Mitigations
- FCS/Ionide.ProjInfo may have .NET version constraints; verify compatibility early.

---

## Work Package WP02: F# RDF Foundation (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Create the F# wrapper layer over dotNetRdf types and the extraction state persistence model.
**Independent Test**: RDF wrapper functions compile and pass unit tests; extraction state can be serialized and deserialized.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP02-fsharp-rdf-foundation.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~400 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-022

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T008 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Rdf/FSharpRdf.fs` — F# wrappers for dotNetRdf (Option conversions, DU node type wrapper, graph creation helpers)
- [ ] T009 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/State/ExtractionState.fs` — extraction state record type, JSON serialization, persist/load to `obj/frank-cli/`
- [ ] T010 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/State/DiffEngine.fs` — compare two extraction states, produce structured diff
- [ ] T011 Create shared vocabulary constants module — Schema.org, Hydra, OWL, SHACL namespace URIs and common terms
- [ ] T012 Unit tests for FSharpRdf helpers and ExtractionState round-trip serialization

### Implementation Notes
- The F# RDF wrapper should make dotNetRdf feel idiomatic: `createUriNode`, `createLiteral`, `assertTriple` as pipeline-friendly functions.
- ExtractionState serializes to JSON (the ontology/shapes graphs serialize as Turtle within the state).
- DiffEngine compares two IGraph instances and categorizes changes (added/removed/modified triples).

### Parallel Opportunities
- T010 (DiffEngine) and T011 (vocabulary constants) can proceed in parallel once T008/T009 are done.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01.

### Risks & Mitigations
- dotNetRdf's mutable C# API may lead to unexpected behavior in F#; wrap early to contain.

---

## Work Package WP03: AST & Type Analysis (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Implement FCS-based analyzers that extract type definitions and route templates from F# source.
**Independent Test**: Analyzers correctly enumerate DUs, records, routes, and HTTP handlers from fixture F# files.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP03-ast-type-analysis.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~500 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-007

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T013 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Analysis/AstAnalyzer.fs` — untyped AST walker for route templates, CE structure detection, and HTTP method handler detection (pattern: `SynExpr.App(Ident "resource", Const(String route))`)
- [ ] T014 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Analysis/TypeAnalyzer.fs` — typed AST walker for DU cases, record fields, option types, list/array types via FSharpEntity
- [ ] T016 Create project loading module using Ionide.ProjInfo — load .fsproj, resolve source files, produce FSharpProjectOptions (no compiled assembly required)
- [ ] T017 Create fixture Frank .fs files for testing (DU types, records, routes, handlers) and unit tests for both analyzers

### Implementation Notes
- AstAnalyzer follows the pattern proven in `src/Frank.Analyzers/DuplicateHandlerAnalyzer.fs` — use SyntaxCollectorBase or manual recursive descent on ParsedInput. AstAnalyzer also detects HTTP method handlers (get, post, put, delete) from CE body nodes.
- TypeAnalyzer uses `FSharpCheckProjectResults.AssemblySignature.Entities` to enumerate types.
- No compiled assembly needed — FCS type-checks source files directly via Ionide.ProjInfo project options.
- Project loading via Ionide.ProjInfo: `WorkspaceLoader.Create(toolsPath)` → `loader.LoadProjects(["proj.fsproj"])`.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T013 (AST) and T014 (typed) can proceed in parallel as they analyze different aspects.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01.

### Risks & Mitigations
- FCS API surface is large and sparsely documented; reference existing DuplicateHandlerAnalyzer for patterns.
- Ionide.ProjInfo requires MSBuild to be installed; document in prerequisites.
- No compiled assembly required — FCS works directly from source files via project options.

---

## Work Package WP04: OWL/SHACL Mapping Engine (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Implement the mapping logic that transforms analyzed F# types/routes into OWL ontology and SHACL shapes.
**Independent Test**: Given known F# type structures, produce correct OWL classes/properties and SHACL shapes.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP04-owl-shacl-mapping.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~500 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-002, FR-003, FR-004, FR-005, FR-006, FR-007

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T018 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Extraction/TypeMapper.fs` — map F# DUs to OWL class hierarchies, records to OWL classes with datatype/object properties
- [ ] T019 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Extraction/RouteMapper.fs` — map Frank route definitions to RDF resource identities with base URI namespace
- [ ] T020 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Extraction/CapabilityMapper.fs` — map HTTP method handlers to Schema.org Actions + Hydra Operations
- [ ] T021 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Extraction/ShapeGenerator.fs` — generate SHACL NodeShapes from F# type constraints (option → minCount, list → maxCount)
- [ ] T022 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Extraction/VocabularyAligner.fs` — align extracted concepts to schema.org/Hydra terms where matches exist
- [ ] T023 Unit tests for each mapper with known input types and expected OWL/SHACL output

### Implementation Notes
- TypeMapper uses dotNetRdf.Ontology's OntologyGraph for building the class hierarchy.
- RouteMapper derives URIs from `{baseUri}/{routeTemplate}`, handling route parameters as URI templates.
- CapabilityMapper creates `schema:Action` subclasses per HTTP method and `hydra:supportedOperation` entries.
- ShapeGenerator creates `sh:NodeShape` with `sh:property` constraints derived from record fields.
- VocabularyAligner does a post-processing pass, replacing generic property URIs with schema.org equivalents where meaningful.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T018-T022 can all proceed in parallel as they are independent mapping concerns.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP02 (RDF foundation) and WP03 (analysis output types).

### Risks & Mitigations
- OWL/SHACL generation correctness is hard to verify without deep RDF knowledge; validate with existing OWL validators.

---

## Work Package WP05: CLI Commands — Extract & Clarify (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Implement the extract and clarify commands that form the first half of the CLI workflow loop.
**Independent Test**: `frank-cli extract --project <path>` produces valid JSON output and persists state; `frank-cli clarify` returns structured ambiguity questions.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP05-cli-extract-clarify.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~400 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-007, FR-008, FR-013, FR-014

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T024 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Commands/ExtractCommand.fs` — orchestrate project loading → AST analysis → type analysis → mapping → state persistence (no compiled assembly required)
- [ ] T025 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Commands/ClarifyCommand.fs` — load extraction state, detect ambiguities (unmapped types, ambiguous DU semantics, missing relationships), produce structured JSON questions
- [ ] T026 Implement JSON output formatting module — shared serialization for all command outputs per contracts/cli-commands.md schemas
- [ ] T027 Implement `--text` human-readable output mode for extract and clarify
- [ ] T028 Unit tests for extract orchestration (mock analyzers) and clarify ambiguity detection

### Implementation Notes
- ExtractCommand orchestrates the pipeline: project load (Ionide.ProjInfo) → AST analysis (routes/handlers) → type analysis (DUs/records) → mappers → state persistence. No compiled assembly required.
- ClarifyCommand analyzes the extraction state for: unmapped types, types with ambiguous semantics, missing cross-type relationships, DUs that could be open or closed enumerations.
- JSON output must match the schemas defined in `contracts/cli-commands.md`.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T026 (JSON formatting) and T027 (text output) can proceed in parallel.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP04 (mapping engine).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Ambiguity detection heuristics may need tuning; start with obvious cases (unmapped types, option vs nullable).

---

## Work Package WP06: CLI Commands — Validate, Diff, Compile (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Implement the validate, diff, and compile commands that complete the CLI workflow, plus the CLI entry point.
**Independent Test**: Full CLI workflow runs end-to-end: extract → clarify → validate → diff → compile produces artifacts in obj/.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP06-cli-validate-diff-compile.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~450 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-009, FR-010, FR-011, FR-012

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T029 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Commands/ValidateCommand.fs` — check completeness (coverage %), consistency, vocabulary alignment, staleness detection
- [ ] T030 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Commands/DiffCommand.fs` — wrap DiffEngine, format structured changes per contract schema
- [ ] T031 Create `src/Frank.Cli.Core/Commands/CompileCommand.fs` — generate OWL/XML + SHACL Turtle + manifest.json to `obj/frank-cli/`, set up embedded resource metadata
- [ ] T032 Create `src/Frank.Cli/Program.fs` — System.CommandLine dispatch for all 5 commands with common `--project` and `--text` options
- [ ] T033 Configure `src/Frank.Cli/Frank.Cli.fsproj` for dotnet tool packaging (PackAsTool, ToolCommandName)
- [ ] T034 Unit tests for validate, diff, compile commands and integration test for full CLI workflow

### Implementation Notes
- ValidateCommand computes coverage metrics, runs consistency checks (dangling references, orphaned properties), and compares source hash against extraction hash for staleness.
- CompileCommand uses dotNetRdf writers: `PrettyRdfXmlWriter` for OWL/XML, `CompressingTurtleWriter` for SHACL shapes, `System.Text.Json` for manifest.
- Program.fs uses System.CommandLine's `RootCommand` with subcommands for each CLI command.
- Tool packaging: `<PackAsTool>true</PackAsTool>`, `<ToolCommandName>frank-cli</ToolCommandName>`.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T029, T030, T031 can proceed in parallel.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP05 (extract/clarify must exist for the workflow).

### Risks & Mitigations
- System.CommandLine API may change between preview/stable; pin version.

---

## Work Package WP07: Frank.LinkedData — Core Runtime (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Implement the core runtime for loading semantic definitions and projecting handler return types to RDF.
**Independent Test**: GraphLoader loads embedded resources into IGraph; InstanceProjector converts F# records to RDF triples.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP07-linkeddata-core.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~400 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-015, FR-020, FR-021

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T035 Create `src/Frank.LinkedData/Rdf/GraphLoader.fs` — load ontology.owl.xml, shapes.shacl.ttl, manifest.json from embedded assembly resources using dotNetRdf's parsers
- [ ] T036 Create `src/Frank.LinkedData/Rdf/InstanceProjector.fs` — reflect on handler return type, map fields to ontology properties, build instance IGraph
- [ ] T037 Create `src/Frank.LinkedData/LinkedDataConfig.fs` — runtime config record, startup validation (fail if embedded resources missing per FR-021)
- [ ] T038 Unit tests for GraphLoader (with test embedded resources) and InstanceProjector (with known record types)

### Implementation Notes
- GraphLoader uses `Assembly.GetManifestResourceStream()` + dotNetRdf parsers (`RdfXmlParser` for OWL, `TurtleParser` for SHACL).
- InstanceProjector uses reflection to enumerate public properties/fields, maps each to a predicate URI from the ontology, creates literal/uri nodes based on property type.
- LinkedDataConfig is loaded once at startup and cached; it validates resource presence and parses the manifest.
- Embedded resource names follow the convention: `Frank.Semantic.ontology.owl.xml`, etc.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T035 (GraphLoader) and T036 (InstanceProjector) can proceed in parallel.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP02 (RDF foundation for shared types/helpers).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Reflection-based serialization must handle nested records, DUs, option types, and collections correctly.

---

## Work Package WP08: Frank.LinkedData — Content Negotiation (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Implement ASP.NET Core output formatters for the three semantic media types and wire them into Frank's resource builder.
**Independent Test**: A Frank resource with `linkedData` enabled serves JSON-LD, Turtle, and RDF/XML via Accept header negotiation.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP08-linkeddata-negotiation.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~450 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-016, FR-017, FR-018, FR-019

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T039 Create `src/Frank.LinkedData/Negotiation/JsonLdFormatter.fs` — ASP.NET Core OutputFormatter for `application/ld+json` using `JsonLdWriter`
- [ ] T040 [P] Create `src/Frank.LinkedData/Negotiation/TurtleFormatter.fs` — OutputFormatter for `text/turtle` using `CompressingTurtleWriter`
- [ ] T041 [P] Create `src/Frank.LinkedData/Negotiation/RdfXmlFormatter.fs` — OutputFormatter for `application/rdf+xml` using `PrettyRdfXmlWriter`
- [ ] T042 Create `src/Frank.LinkedData/ResourceBuilderExtensions.fs` — `linkedData` custom operation on ResourceBuilder (follows Frank.Auth/Frank.OpenApi pattern)
- [ ] T043 Create `src/Frank.LinkedData/WebHostBuilderExtensions.fs` — `useLinkedData` service registration, formatter registration, startup validation
- [ ] T044 Unit tests for formatters (verify correct RDF output) and integration tests for content negotiation (Accept header routing)

### Implementation Notes
- Each formatter extends `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Formatters.OutputFormatter`, sets supported media types, and uses dotNetRdf writers.
- ResourceBuilderExtensions follows the AutoOpen pattern from Frank.Auth — adds metadata convention to ResourceSpec that signals LinkedData is enabled.
- WebHostBuilderExtensions registers the formatters via `MvcOptions.OutputFormatters`, loads LinkedDataConfig, and performs startup validation.
- Content negotiation must NOT interfere with existing formatters — JSON-LD only matches `application/ld+json`, not `application/json`.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T039, T040, T041 (all three formatters) can proceed in parallel.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP07 (core runtime for GraphLoader/InstanceProjector).

### Risks & Mitigations
- ASP.NET Core formatter pipeline ordering matters; ensure semantic formatters are added after default formatters.

---

## Work Package WP09: Frank.Cli.MSBuild Package (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Create the MSBuild integration package that auto-embeds semantic artifacts from obj/frank-cli/ into the compiled assembly.
**Independent Test**: Adding `<PackageReference Include="Frank.Cli.MSBuild" />` to a project causes files in `obj/frank-cli/` to be embedded as assembly resources after `dotnet build`.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP09-msbuild-package.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~300 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-012

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T045 Create `src/Frank.Cli.MSBuild/build/Frank.Cli.MSBuild.props` — default property definitions (intermediate output path for frank-cli)
- [ ] T046 Create `src/Frank.Cli.MSBuild/build/Frank.Cli.MSBuild.targets` — target to auto-include `obj/frank-cli/*.owl.xml`, `*.shacl.ttl`, `*.json` as EmbeddedResource
- [ ] T047 Create `src/Frank.Cli.MSBuild/buildTransitive/Frank.Cli.MSBuild.targets` — transitive target for indirect consumers
- [ ] T048 Configure `src/Frank.Cli.MSBuild/Frank.Cli.MSBuild.csproj` for NuGet packaging (include build/ and buildTransitive/ in package)
- [ ] T049 Add build-time validation target — emit warning if `obj/frank-cli/` directory is empty or missing when package is referenced

### Implementation Notes
- .props file sets `FrankCliOutputPath` property defaulting to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)frank-cli/`.
- .targets file includes a `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` target that uses `<ItemGroup>` to include artifacts as `<EmbeddedResource>` with `LogicalName` prefix `Frank.Semantic.`.
- Validation target runs `BeforeTargets="Build"` and uses `Condition="!Exists('$(FrankCliOutputPath)')"` to emit MSBuild warning.
- Package .csproj uses `<None Include="build/**" Pack="true" PackagePath="build/" />` pattern.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T045-T048 are sequential within the package but the entire WP can proceed in parallel with WP07/WP08.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP06 (compile command defines the artifact output format and naming).

### Risks & Mitigations
- MSBuild target timing is subtle; test with both single-target and multi-target consuming projects.

---

## Work Package WP10: CLI Core Tests (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Comprehensive unit tests for the extraction and mapping logic in Frank.Cli.Core.
**Independent Test**: All tests pass with `dotnet test test/Frank.Cli.Core.Tests/`.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP10-cli-core-tests.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~400 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-002, FR-003, FR-004, FR-005, FR-006, FR-007, FR-008, FR-009, FR-010, FR-011

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T050 Create TypeMapper tests — DU → OWL class hierarchy, record → OWL class with properties, field types → datatype/object properties
- [ ] T051 [P] Create RouteMapper tests — route templates → RDF resource URIs, base URI overrides, route parameter handling
- [ ] T052 [P] Create CapabilityMapper tests — HTTP methods → Schema.org Actions + Hydra Operations
- [ ] T053 [P] Create ShapeGenerator tests — option → minCount 0, required → minCount 1, list → unbounded maxCount
- [ ] T054 Create AstAnalyzer tests with fixture .fs files — detect `resource "..."` invocations, HTTP method operations, nested CEs

### Implementation Notes
- Use Expecto `testList` and `testCase` pattern matching existing Frank.Tests conventions.
- Fixture .fs files should cover: simple DU, multi-case DU, nested record, optional fields, list fields, multiple resources with different HTTP methods.
- Tests should verify the RDF output using dotNetRdf's graph querying (e.g., `graph.GetTriplesWithSubject(uri)`) rather than string comparison.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T050-T054 can all proceed in parallel.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP06 (all CLI core code must exist to test).

### Risks & Mitigations
- FCS-dependent tests (T054) require fixture projects that compile; keep fixtures minimal.

---

## Work Package WP11: LinkedData Tests (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: Comprehensive unit and integration tests for Frank.LinkedData.
**Independent Test**: All tests pass with `dotnet test test/Frank.LinkedData.Tests/`.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP11-linkeddata-tests.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~400 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-015, FR-016, FR-017, FR-018, FR-019, FR-020, FR-021

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T055 Create InstanceProjector tests — record → RDF triples, DU → typed triples, option handling, nested types
- [ ] T056 [P] Create formatter tests — verify each formatter produces valid JSON-LD/Turtle/RDF/XML output for known input graphs
- [ ] T057 Create content negotiation integration tests — TestHost with `linkedData` resource, verify Accept header routing
- [ ] T058 Create startup validation tests — verify error on missing embedded resources, success on present resources

### Implementation Notes
- Use ASP.NET Core TestHost (`Microsoft.AspNetCore.TestHost`) for integration tests, matching existing Frank.Tests patterns.
- Formatter tests should parse the output back using dotNetRdf parsers to verify round-trip correctness.
- For embedded resource tests, create test assemblies with/without embedded semantic definitions.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T055, T056 can proceed in parallel.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP08 (all LinkedData code must exist to test).

### Risks & Mitigations
- TestHost setup may need careful configuration for content negotiation; reference existing Frank.Tests for patterns.

---

## Work Package WP12: Sample App & End-to-End Integration (Priority: P3) 🎯 MVP Validation

**Goal**: Create the LinkedData sample application and validate the complete extract-to-serve pipeline end-to-end.
**Independent Test**: `frank-cli extract → compile → dotnet build → curl` serves all three semantic media types from the sample app.
**Prompt**: `tasks/WP12-sample-e2e.md`
**Estimated Size**: ~350 lines
**Requirement Refs**: FR-013, FR-014, FR-015, FR-016, FR-017, FR-018

### Included Subtasks
- [ ] T059 Create `sample/Frank.LinkedData.Sample/Program.fs` — sample Frank app with typed resources, DU models, and `linkedData` enabled
- [ ] T060 Run full CLI pipeline against sample: extract → clarify → validate → compile
- [ ] T061 Verify embedded resources in compiled sample assembly and test all three media types via HTTP
- [ ] T062 Validate quickstart.md instructions against actual sample workflow
- [ ] T063 Final solution integration — verify `dotnet build Frank.sln` builds everything, existing tests still pass (SC-003)

### Implementation Notes
- Sample app should define 2-3 resources with typed models (records, DUs) to exercise the full extraction pipeline.
- E2E test can be a shell script or Expecto test that orchestrates the full workflow.
- Quickstart validation ensures the documented developer workflow matches reality.
- SC-003 verification: run existing Frank test suite to confirm zero behavioral changes.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T062 (quickstart validation) can proceed in parallel with T060/T061.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP08 (LinkedData formatters), WP09 (MSBuild package), WP06 (CLI tool).

### Risks & Mitigations
- E2E pipeline involves many moving parts; isolate failures by testing each stage independently first.

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

```
WP01 (Scaffolding)
  ├──▶ WP02 (RDF Foundation)
  │      ├──▶ WP04 (OWL/SHACL Mapping) ← also needs WP03
  │      └──▶ WP07 (LinkedData Core)
  │              └──▶ WP08 (LinkedData Negotiation)
  │                     ├──▶ WP11 (LinkedData Tests)
  │                     └──▶ WP12 (Sample & E2E) ← also needs WP06, WP09
  └──▶ WP03 (AST & Type Analysis)
         └──▶ WP04 (OWL/SHACL Mapping)
                └──▶ WP05 (Extract & Clarify)
                       └──▶ WP06 (Validate/Diff/Compile)
                              ├──▶ WP09 (MSBuild Package)
                              └──▶ WP10 (CLI Core Tests)
```

- **Parallel after WP01**: WP02 ∥ WP03
- **Parallel after WP02**: WP07 (LinkedData) can proceed in parallel with WP04 (CLI mapping)
- **Parallel after WP06**: WP09 ∥ WP10 (MSBuild and tests independent)
- **Parallel after WP08**: WP11 (LinkedData tests independent of CLI tests)

- **MVP Scope**: WP01 → WP02 → WP03 → WP04 → WP05 → WP06 → WP12 (CLI pipeline + basic E2E)
- **Full scope adds**: WP07 → WP08 (LinkedData runtime), WP09 (MSBuild), WP10 + WP11 (comprehensive tests)

---
